screenPrinting.q: What's the minimum order for screen printing?
screenPrinting.a: 10 pieces of the same design. Below 10 pieces, DTF is more cost-effective.

screenPrinting.q: How many colors can you print?
screenPrinting.a: Up to 6 spot colors per design.

screenPrinting.q: Can I supply my own blanks?
screenPrinting.a: Yes. Turnaround stays at 3 business days.

screenPrinting.q: Can you print on dark fabric?
screenPrinting.a: Yes. Light-on-dark requires an extra screen for surface coverage on dark textile, which we include in the quote — no separate "underbase" line item.
Standard for light fabrics. Available for dark fabrics on request.
Yes: puff, metallic, foil, glow-in-the-dark. Available from 20 pieces.

screenPrinting.q: How long does a screen-printed garment last?
screenPrinting.a: A correctly cured screen print survives 100+ industrial washes without significant fading or cracking.
Setup is included in the per-piece price. There is no separate setup fee. Reorders of the same artwork skip the setup phase entirely.

screenPrinting.q: Can you print across the chest, back, and sleeves?
screenPrinting.a: Yes. Multi-location prints are quoted by total print area, not per location.

screenPrinting.q: What file format do you need for screen printing?
screenPrinting.a: Vector files (AI, EPS, PDF) at the size you want printed. If you only have a raster file, we can vectorise it for a one-time fee.

embroidery.q: What's the minimum order for embroidery?
How many thread colors can you use?
Can you embroider on caps, hoodies, polos?
Can you embroider on the side of finished trousers?
embroidery.a: No. Side-leg embroidery requires the panel to be sewn after embroidery — only possible during garment production, not on finished items. For finished pants, we recommend a puff print as the alternative.
Yes, available for high-relief lettering. Best on flat surfaces — caps, hoodies, sweatshirts.
A correctly digitised and stitched logo survives 100+ industrial washes.

embroidery.q: Do you charge a setup fee?
embroidery.a: A one-time digitising fee of €10–20 per design. After that, no setup costs on reorders.

embroidery.q: What file format do you need?
embroidery.a: Vector (AI, EPS, PDF, SVG) preferred. We can work from a clean raster file too, but vector gives the best stitch result.
Letters down to 4 mm in height. Below that they become unreadable in thread.

embroidery.q: Do you embroider on customer-supplied garments?

dtf.q: What's the minimum order for DTF?
How does DTF feel on the garment?
dtf.a: A thin film, smooth to the touch. Stretches with the fabric.

dtf.q: Does DTF crack or peel over time?
dtf.a: Not when applied correctly. We test every batch before sending and use industrial-grade film and inks rated for 50+ washes.

dtf.q: What file format do you need?
dtf.a: PNG, JPG, or PDF at 300 DPI. Higher resolution is better — DTF reproduces every detail in the source file.

dtf.q: Can you do white ink on dark garments?
dtf.a: Yes. White underbase is applied automatically on dark fabrics. Included in the price.

dtf.q: Can DTF be applied to any fabric?
dtf.a: Cotton, polyester, blends, canvas, nylon — yes. Stretch fabrics like Lycra and elastane require a stretch-rated adhesive, which we use by default.

dtf.q: How many DTF prints can you produce per day?

dtf.a: On request, for orders under 20 pieces. Subject to current schedule.
